questions.text: What is the sum of angles on a straight line?
questions.explanation: Angles on a straight line add up to 180°.

questions.text: If two lines are parallel and cut by a transversal, what can be said about corresponding angles?
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles formed by a transversal with parallel lines are equal.

questions.text: Which of the following pairs of angles are alternate interior angles?

options.a: Angles on the same side of transversal and inside the lines
options.b: Angles on opposite sides of transversal and outside the lines
options.c: Angles on opposite sides of transversal and inside the lines
options.d: Angles on the same side of transversal and outside the lines

questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles lie between the two lines and on opposite sides of the transversal.

questions.text: What is the measure of co-interior angles when two parallel lines are cut by a transversal?
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles are supplementary, meaning their sum is 180°.

questions.text: If two lines are perpendicular, what is the measure of the angle between them?
questions.explanation: Perpendicular lines meet at a right angle, which is 90°.

questions.text: When a transversal cuts two parallel lines, which angles are always equal?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal when lines are parallel.

questions.text: If two lines are parallel, which of these is true?
questions.explanation: Parallel lines never meet no matter how far they are extended.

questions.text: Which angle pair forms a linear pair?

options.a: Two adjacent angles on a straight line

questions.explanation: A linear pair consists of two adjacent angles that add up to 180° on a straight line.

questions.text: If a transversal cuts two lines and the alternate interior angles are equal, what can be concluded about the lines?
questions.explanation: Equal alternate interior angles indicate the lines are parallel.

questions.text: Which of the following is a condition for two lines to be parallel?
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles being equal is a condition for lines to be parallel.

questions.text: What do we call the line that cuts two or more lines?
questions.explanation: A transversal is a line that intersects two or more lines at distinct points.

questions.text: If two lines are cut by a transversal and the sum of co-interior angles is 180°, what can be said about the lines?
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles summing to 180° means the lines are parallel.

questions.text: Which angle is formed between a transversal and a line when they meet at 90°?
questions.explanation: An angle of 90° formed between two lines is called a right angle.

questions.text: If two lines are perpendicular, what is true about their slopes (if drawn on a coordinate plane)?
questions.explanation: Perpendicular lines have slopes that are negative reciprocals of each other.

questions.text: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one corresponding angle measures 65°, what is the measure of the other corresponding angle?
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal when lines are parallel.

questions.text: Lines l and m are cut by a transversal t. If an alternate interior angle is 110°, what is the measure of the corresponding angle on the same side of the transversal?
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles are supplementary; 180° - 110° = 70°.

questions.text: A transversal cuts two parallel lines. If one co-interior angle is 120°, what is the measure of the other co-interior angle on the same side?
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles add up to 180°, so 180° - 120° = 60°.

questions.text: If two lines are perpendicular, and one angle formed by them is 90°, what is the measure of the adjacent angle formed?
questions.explanation: Adjacent angles formed by perpendicular lines are also 90° since they form right angles.

questions.text: Two lines are cut by a transversal. If the corresponding angles are not equal, what can be concluded?
questions.explanation: If corresponding angles are not equal, the lines are not parallel.

questions.text: A transversal cuts two lines such that one alternate interior angle is 75°. What must be the measure of the other alternate interior angle for the lines to be parallel?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles must be equal for the lines to be parallel.

questions.text: A railway track has two parallel rails. A third rail is laid such that it crosses the two rails forming alternate interior angles of 65°. What is the measure of the corresponding angle formed on the other side of the transversal?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles formed by a transversal with parallel lines are equal. Corresponding angles are also equal to alternate interior angles, so the corresponding angle is 65°.

questions.text: In a classroom, two parallel walls are connected by a beam. The beam acts as a transversal making co-interior angles of 110° and x°. Find the value of x.
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles on the same side of the transversal are supplementary, so x + 110° = 180°. Therefore, x = 70°.

questions.text: A rectangular garden has two parallel sides AB and DC. A path crosses the garden forming a transversal that makes an angle of 40° with side AB. What is the measure of the alternate interior angle on the opposite side of the transversal?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles formed by a transversal with parallel lines are equal, so the alternate interior angle is 40°.

questions.text: A ladder leans against a wall making an angle of 75° with the ground. The ground and the wall are perpendicular. What is the measure of the angle between the ladder and the wall?
questions.explanation: The ground and wall are perpendicular (90°). The ladder makes 75° with the ground, so the angle with the wall is 90° - 75° = 15°.

questions.text: Two railway tracks are parallel. A signal post stands such that the angle between the post and one track is 60°. What is the angle between the post and the other track if the post acts as a transversal?
questions.explanation: The post acts as a transversal to parallel tracks. Co-interior angles are supplementary, so the angle with the other track is 180° - 60° = 120°.

questions.text: A street has two parallel walls. A signboard is placed such that it forms a transversal making corresponding angles of 55° with one wall. Find the angle it makes with the other wall.
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles formed by a transversal with parallel lines are equal, so the angle with the other wall is 55°.

questions.text: A rectangular field has two parallel sides. A sprinkler pipe crosses the field forming a transversal. If one of the alternate interior angles is 35°, what is the measure of the other alternate interior angle?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles formed by a transversal with parallel lines are equal, so the other alternate interior angle is 35°.

questions.text: Two parallel roads are crossed by a streetlight pole acting as a transversal. If the angle between the pole and one road is 80°, what is the angle between the pole and the other road?
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles formed by a transversal with parallel lines are supplementary, so the angle is 180° - 80° = 100°.

questions.text: A flagpole stands perpendicular to the ground. A wire is tied from the top of the pole to a point on the ground making an angle of 60° with the ground. What is the angle between the wire and the pole?
questions.explanation: The pole and ground are perpendicular (90°). The wire makes 60° with the ground, so the angle with the pole is 90° - 60° = 30°.

questions.text: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one of the corresponding angles is 70°, find the measure of the co-interior angle adjacent to it on the same side of the transversal.
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles on the same side of the transversal are supplementary, so the adjacent co-interior angle is 180° - 70° = 110°.

questions.text: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one of the alternate interior angles is 65°, find the measure of the corresponding angle and the co-interior angle on the same side of the transversal.
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal, so the corresponding angle is also 65°. Co-interior angles are supplementary, so 180° - 65° = 115°.

questions.text: A transversal intersects two lines such that one pair of corresponding angles is equal. If one of these angles measures (3x + 10)° and the other measures (5x - 14)°, find the value of x and determine if the lines are parallel.
questions.explanation: Since corresponding angles are equal, 3x + 10 = 5x - 14. Solving gives 2x = 24, so x = 12. Therefore, lines are parallel as corresponding angles are equal.

questions.text: Two lines are cut by a transversal. One of the co-interior angles is (2x + 20)° and the other is (3x - 10)°. Find x and determine if the lines are parallel.
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles are supplementary, so (2x + 20) + (3x - 10) = 180. Simplify: 5x + 10 = 180, 5x = 170, x = 34. Since co-interior angles sum to 180°, lines are parallel.

questions.text: A transversal intersects two lines. If one alternate interior angle is (4x - 5)° and the corresponding angle is (2x + 15)°, find x and state whether the lines are parallel or not.
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal if lines are parallel. So, 4x - 5 = 2x + 15, 2x = 20, x = 10. Lines are parallel.

questions.text: Two lines are cut by a transversal. If the sum of one pair of corresponding angles is 150°, what can you say about the lines? Justify your answer by calculating the measures of the angles if one angle is (x + 30)° and the other is (2x + 20)°.

options.a: Lines are not parallel; angles are 50° and 100°
options.b: Lines are parallel; angles are 60° and 90°
options.c: Lines are not parallel; angles are 40° and 110°
options.d: Lines are parallel; angles are 70° and 80°

questions.explanation: Sum of corresponding angles is 150°, so lines are not parallel (they should be equal). Solve x + 30 + 2x + 20 = 150; 3x + 50 = 150; 3x = 100; x = 100/3 ≈ 33.33. Angles: 33.33 + 30 = 63.33°, 2*33.33 + 20 = 86.66°, sum ≈ 150°, lines not parallel.

questions.text: In a figure, two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one angle adjacent to the transversal is (3x + 10)° and its alternate interior angle is (5x - 30)°, find x and the measure of the angle adjacent to the transversal.
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal: 3x + 10 = 5x - 30, 2x = 40, x = 20. Angle adjacent to transversal = 3*20 + 10 = 70°.

questions.text: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one of the alternate interior angles is 65°, find the measures of the corresponding angle and the co-interior angle on the same side of the transversal.
accepted_forms: Corresponding angle is 65 degrees and co-interior angle is 115 degrees
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal, so corresponding angle = 65°. Co-interior angles are supplementary, so co-interior angle = 180° - 65° = 115°.

questions.text: Lines l and m are parallel and are cut by a transversal t. If one of the corresponding angles is (3x + 10)° and the alternate interior angle is (5x - 14)°, find the value of x and the measure of each angle.
questions.correct_answer: x = 12°, Corresponding angle = 46°, Alternate interior angle = 46°
accepted_forms: x=12, Corresponding angle=46°, Alternate interior angle=46°
x = 12 degrees, angles are 46 degrees each
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal, so 3x + 10 = 5x - 14. Solving: 2x = 24, x = 12. Substitute x: 3(12)+10=46°, 5(12)-14=46°.

questions.text: A transversal cuts two parallel lines. If one of the co-interior angles is (2x + 20)°, and the other co-interior angle is (3x - 10)°, find x and the measure of each angle.
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles are supplementary, so (2x + 20) + (3x - 10) = 180. Simplify: 5x + 10 = 180, 5x = 170, x = 34. However, checking calculations: 2x+20=2(30)+20=80°, 3x-10=3(30)-10=80°, sum=160°, so recheck. Correct equation: 2x + 20 + 3x - 10 = 180 => 5x + 10 = 180 => 5x = 170 => x = 34. So angles are 2(34)+20=88°, 3(34)-10=92°, sum 180°. Correct answer: x=34°, angles 88° and 92°.

questions.text: Two lines are cut by a transversal such that one of the corresponding angles is 70° and the alternate interior angle on the other side of the transversal is 110°. Are the two lines parallel? Justify your answer.
questions.correct_answer: No, the lines are not parallel because corresponding angles are not equal (70° ≠ 110°).
accepted_forms: No, lines are not parallel because corresponding angles are not equal
No, not parallel as corresponding angles are unequal
questions.explanation: For lines to be parallel, corresponding angles must be equal. Here, 70° ≠ 110°, so lines are not parallel.

questions.text: A line perpendicular to one of two parallel lines is drawn. What is the angle between this perpendicular line and the other parallel line? Explain.
questions.correct_answer: 90°; the perpendicular line forms right angles with both parallel lines.
accepted_forms: 90 degrees; perpendicular to one parallel line is perpendicular to the other
90°, because perpendicular to one parallel line is perpendicular to both
Right angle; perpendicular line forms 90° with both parallel lines
questions.explanation: Parallel lines are equidistant and never meet. A line perpendicular to one parallel line is also perpendicular to the other, so the angle is 90°.

questions.text: If two lines are cut by a transversal such that the sum of the co-interior angles is 150°, are the lines parallel? Explain your answer.
questions.correct_answer: No; co-interior angles must be supplementary (sum 180°) for lines to be parallel.
accepted_forms: No, because co-interior angles sum is not 180°
No, lines are not parallel as co-interior angles do not add up to 180°
No; co-interior angles must sum to 180° for parallel lines
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles on the same side of a transversal are supplementary only if lines are parallel. Sum 150° ≠ 180°, so lines are not parallel.

questions.text: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One of the angles formed is (4x + 5)° and its corresponding angle is (6x - 15)°. Find x and the value of each angle.
accepted_forms: x = 10 degrees, both angles are 45 degrees
questions.explanation: Corresponding angles are equal: 4x + 5 = 6x - 15. Simplify: 2x = 20, x = 10. Substitute: 4(10)+5=45°, 6(10)-15=45°.

questions.text: A transversal intersects two parallel lines. One of the alternate interior angles is (7x - 20)° and its corresponding angle is (5x + 10)°. Find x and the measure of each angle.
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal, so 7x - 20 = 5x + 10. Simplify: 2x = 30, x = 15. Substitute: 7(15)-20=85°, 5(15)+10=85°.

questions.text: Two lines are cut by a transversal. If the alternate interior angles are equal and one of the angles is 3x°, and the corresponding angle is (2x + 30)°, find x and the measure of each angle.
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are equal: 3x = 2x + 30. Simplify: x = 30. Substitute: 3(30)=90°, 2(30)+30=90°.

questions.text: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. If one angle is (x + 40)° and its co-interior angle is (3x - 20)°, find x and the measure of each angle.
questions.explanation: Co-interior angles are supplementary: (x + 40) + (3x - 20) = 180. Simplify: 4x + 20 = 180, 4x = 160, x = 40. Substitute: 40+40=80°, 3(40)-20=100°.

questions.case_text: In a city park, two parallel roads, Road A and Road B, run east-west and are connected by a transversal footpath crossing them at an angle. A gardener notices that the angles formed where the footpath crosses Road A are 65° and 115°. He wants to use these angles to design benches at the crossing points to ensure symmetry and comfort for park visitors.

subparts.text: What is the measure of the corresponding angle to the 65° angle on Road B where the footpath crosses it?
subparts.explanation: Corresponding angles formed by a transversal crossing parallel lines are equal, so the angle corresponding to 65° on Road B is also 65°.

subparts.text: If the gardener wants to place benches at the co-interior angles formed by the footpath and Road B, what is the sum of these two angles?
subparts.explanation: Co-interior angles on the same side of the transversal add up to 180° when the lines are parallel.

subparts.text: Calculate the measure of the alternate interior angle to the 115° angle on Road A formed by the footpath.
subparts.explanation: Alternate interior angles between parallel lines are equal, so the alternate interior angle to 115° is also 115°.

questions.case_text: A railway engineer is designing two parallel railway tracks connected by a maintenance walkway acting as a transversal. At the point where the walkway crosses the first track, the angle between the walkway and the track is measured as 72°. The engineer needs to calculate various angles to ensure safety railings are installed correctly along the walkway.

subparts.text: What is the measure of the angle vertically opposite to the 72° angle at the first track?
subparts.explanation: Vertically opposite angles are equal, so the angle opposite to 72° is also 72°.

subparts.text: If the walkway crosses the second track (parallel to the first), what is the measure of the corresponding angle to 72° at the second track?
subparts.explanation: Corresponding angles formed by a transversal crossing parallel lines are equal, so the corresponding angle is 72°.

subparts.text: Find the measure of the co-interior angle on the same side of the walkway at the second track, corresponding to the 72° angle at the first track.
subparts.explanation: Co-interior angles on the same side of the transversal add up to 180°, so 180° - 72° = 108°.
